experiment: cuda-graph on pure-attention A3B MoE single-user decode (RDNA4 2x R9700, TP=2, v0.5.12 + patch-006)
hypothesis: M=1 MoE decode is dispatch-bound (many tiny kernels, ~3B active params) so cuda-graph replay removes the launch gap — unlike the GPU-bound dense models where cuda-graph gave no help

profile_evidence.note: awq_gemv_moe_hip (task #26) would only touch the 20% moe kernel — NOT the lever. cuda-graph is.

coder-reap-25b.note: REAP prune of Coder-30B-A3B, same family

gemma4-26b.note: triton SWA flash captures fine; validate_capabilities 3/3 PASS incl. vision under graph

rollout.mechanism: launch.sh line-44 default --disable-cuda-graph; CUDA_GRAPH_ENABLE=1 env toggles on; the 3 MoE presets set CUDA_GRAPH="" directly
rollout.dense_unaffected: GPU-bound (qwen36-27b 85.8% util) — no launch gap to remove; global default stays OFF

conclusion: cuda-graph is the MoE-decode lever (1.67-2.54x) the way patch-006 batched-load was the dense-GEMV lever. Both found by profiling util/roofline, not guessing. Largest at short ctx, positive through 256K.
